semanticCore: Holding, keeping, shunning, musk, or skin/leather
quranicRelevance: Holding fast to the Book (Tamasuk - 'yumas-sikoon'), holding/retaining, and the sweet perfume musk (Misk).

keyDerivations.meaning: To hold, retain, keep back, withhold, or seize
To hold fast, cling, or adhere to

figurativeOrRareUsages: خِتَامُهُ مِسْكٌ (83:26): Its seal/end is musk (describing the fragrant completion of the drink of Paradise).
discardedNoise: Specific physical methods of grasping ropes.
summaryEn: The root denotes holding and fragrance, representing the duty of holding fast to scriptures (Tamasuk), withholding resources, and the premium fragrance of musk.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الإمساك والطيب، ممثلاً الاستمساك بالحق والوحي (تمسك)، وإمساك الأرزاق والرياح، وطيب المسك الختامي.
rawText: أَمْسَكَ 4 He retained; he withheld. (Msb.) === He maintained: he was tenacious, or niggardly. === He, or it, held fast a thing: and arrested it. === أَمْسَكَهُ He held, retained, detained, restrained, stayed, confined, imprisoned, or withheld, him. (K.) === أَمْسَكَ عَنِ الأَمْرِ He held, refrained, or abstained, from the thing. (Msb.) === أَمْسَكَهُ He grasped it, clutched it, laid hold upon it; or seized it, (قَبَضَ عَلَيْهِ) بِيَدِهِ with his hand: (Msb:) or he took it; or took it with his hand, (أَخَذَهُ,) namely, a rope, &c.: (Mgh:) or he held, or clung, to it: (TA:) [as also 🡻تَمَسكَبِهِ]. Also, أَمْسَكَ بِهِ signifies [the same; or] he laid hold upon, or seized, somewhat of his body, or what might detain him, as an arm or a hand, or a garment, and the like: but أَمْسَقَهُ may signify he withheld him, or restrained him, from acting according to his own free will. (Mugh, art. بِ.) === أَمْسَكَ بَطْنَهُ [It bound, or confined, his belly (or bowels)]: said of medicine. (S, O, Msb, K; all in art. عقل.) === الإِمْسَاك, in relation to تَحْجِيل: see an unusual application of it in art. طلق, conj. 4. اِسْتَمْسَكَ البَطْنُ 10 [The belly (or bowels) became bound, or confined]. (TA in art. عقل.) === اِسْتَمْسَكَ بِهِ [sometimes] He sought to lay hold upon it. (Bd, in ii. 257.) === اِسْتَمْسَكَ: see an ex. voce صِرْعَةٌ. اِمْتَسَكَ بِهِ 8 He clutched, or griped, him, or it; i. q. 🡻تَمَسَّكَبِهِ. (MA.) تَمَاسَكَ 6 He withheld, or restrained, himself: (PS:) he was able, or powerful; as also تَمَالَكَ, q. v. (KL.) === مَا تَمَاسَكَ أَنْ فَعَلَ كَذَا He could not restrain himself from doing so; syn. مَا تَمَالكَ. (S.) === تَمَاسَكَ It held together. === إِنَّهُ لَذُو تَمَاسُكٍ † Verily he possesses intelligence. (TA.) And مَابِهِ تَمَاسُكٌ ‡ There is no good in him. (TA.) See مُسْكَةٌ. تَمَسَّكَ 5 see 4 and 8. === تَمَسَّكَ بِحَبْلِهِ He held fast by his covenant: see أَعْصَمَ. مَسَكٌ Tortoise-shell; syn. ذَبْلٌ: (K:) bracelets made of tortoise-shell (ذَبْلٌ), or of عاج [ivory]: (S, Msb:) bracelets and anklets made of horn and of عاج: n. un. with مُسْكَةٌ. (K.) مَسِيكٌ : see مُسْكَةٌ. مَسَّاكاتٌ [in the CK, art. روض, written مُسّاكات,] Places, in land, or in the ground, to which the rain-water flows, and which retain it. (TA.) See ضَابِطَةٌ. مَسَّكَ بِالنَّارِ 2 : see ثقّب. مُتَمَاسِكٌ Compact in the limbs, (TA in art. بدن,) or flesh. (TA in this art.) مُسْكَانٌ : see art. سكن. مُسْكَةٌ Intelligence: (Msb:) or full intelligence, (K, TA,) and judgment; judgment and intel-ligence to which one has recourse; as also مُسْكٌ, not 🡻مَسِيكٌ, as in the K; (TA;) i. q. تَمَاسُكٌ. (Mgh.) You say, لَيْسَ لَهُ مُسْكَةٌ He has no intel-ligence. (Msb.) === لَيْسَ بِهِ مُسْكَةٌ He has no strength. (Msb.) مُمْسَكٌ , said of a horse, white on both fore and kind leg on the same side: see مُحَجَّلٌ. مِسَاكٌ or مَسَاكٌ A kind of needles: see مِدَادٌ. مِسْكٌ [Musk: it is obtained from the muskdeer, moschus moschiferus; being found in the male animal, in a vesicle near the navel and prepuce.] It is masc. and fem. (IAmb, TA voce ذَكِىٌّ.)
